data.content:
### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.3.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version due to outdated envd usage. It requires a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string): TemplateError
}
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Define TemplateError

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template uses an incompatible old envd version. The constructor accepts a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    // ... implementation
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom Sandbox with Template ID (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Starts a new sandbox instance using a previously built custom template. The template ID is passed to the `Sandbox.create` method in JavaScript.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create({
  template: 'YOUR_TEMPLATE_ID',
})
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.5/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version. It takes a message and an optional stack trace.

```TypeScript
class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
    if (stackTrace) {
      this.stack = stackTrace;
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom Sandbox - JavaScript and Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/page.mdx

Creates and runs a custom sandbox using a template ID. The code examples demonstrate how to import the Sandbox class, create a sandbox instance with a specific template, and execute code within it, leveraging installed dependencies like 'cowsay'.

```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

// Your template ID from the previous step
const templateID = 'id-of-your-template' // $HighlightLine
// Pass the template ID to the `Sandbox.create` method
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create(templateID) // $HighlightLine

// The template installed cowsay, so we can use it
const execution = await sandbox.runCode(`
import cowsay
cowsay.say('Hello from E2B!')
`)

console.log(execution.stdout)
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

# Your template ID from the previous step
template_id = 'id-of-your-template' # $HighlightLine
# Pass the template ID to the `Sandbox.create` method
sandbox = Sandbox.create(template_id) # $HighlightLine

# The template installed cowsay, so we can use it
execution = sandbox.run_code("""
import cowsay
cowsay.say('Hello from E2B!')
""")

print(execution.stdout)
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize New Sandbox Template with SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/template/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox template using the E2B SDK. Allows specifying the path, template name, and target language (typescript, python-sync, python-async).

```bash
e2b template init-v2 [options]
```

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template uses an incompatible old envd version. It requires a string message for instantiation.

```ts
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string)
}
```

### Define TemplateError Class (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, indicating incompatibility with older envd versions. It requires a string message during instantiation.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  message: string;
  constructor(message: string) {
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

The TemplateError is thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version, typically due to using an older envd version. It includes a message and an optional stack trace, guiding users on necessary template updates.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError Class

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.0/errors/page.mdx

The TemplateError class is thrown when a template uses an incompatible, older version of the envd system. It extends SandboxError and requires a string message. This class is defined in errors.ts.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends SandboxError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
  }
}

// Defined in errors.ts:82
```

### Migrate E2B Template to SDK Format using E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.3/template/page.mdx

Migrates e2b.Dockerfile and e2b.toml to the new Template SDK format. No specific options are detailed beyond the command itself.

```bash
e2b template migrate [options]
```

Thrown when a template uses an outdated version of envd, indicating incompatibility with the current SDK. This error inherits from SandboxError.

```typescript

  message: string;

  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

The `create` class method is the recommended way to instantiate and initialize a new sandbox. It allows configuration of various parameters such as template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, debug mode, and request timeout. The default sandbox template is 'base'.

```python
@classmethod
async def create(cls,
                 template: Optional[str] = None,
                 timeout: Optional[int] = None,
                 metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                 domain: Optional[str] = None,
                 debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                 request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)

```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation (Default Template)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the default 'desktop' sandbox template. This is a static method called on the Sandbox class.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox/create (default)

### Description
Creates a new sandbox from the default `desktop` sandbox template. This is a static method.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/sandbox/create`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Connection options for the sandbox.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "opts": {
    "onVideoStreamStart": "(url: string) => void",
    "videoStream": true
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxInstance** (Sandbox) - The newly created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxInstance": "<sandbox-instance-details>"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new sandbox with default or custom parameters

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify a custom template, timeout duration, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, debug mode, sandbox ID, and request timeout. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is provided.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox(template='python-3.11', timeout=600, metadata={'user_id': '123'}, envs={'MY_VAR': 'value'})
print(f'Sandbox created with ID: {sandbox.sandbox_id}')
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when a template uses an incompatible, older version of the envd environment. This error indicates a version mismatch issue in the E2B template system. It expects a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
  }
}
```

### Create a New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new E2B cloud sandbox instance. Users can specify a template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, and API key for authentication. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is provided. Use this class method instead of the constructor.

```python
class AsyncSandbox(SandboxSetup, SandboxApi)

@classmethod
async def create(cls,
                 template: Optional[str] = None,
                 timeout: Optional[int] = None,
                 metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                 api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                 domain: Optional[str] = None,
                 debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)
```

Class method to create a new sandbox instance. Allows customization through parameters like template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, proxy, and internet access. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is specified. Returns the created sandbox instance.

```python

async def create(cls, template: Optional[str] = None, timeout: Optional[int] = None, metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None, proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None, secure: Optional[bool] = None, allow_internet_access: Optional[bool] = True):
    # ... implementation details ...

```

### Create Sandbox from Default Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the default 'desktop' sandbox template. This method is static and requires the 'Sandbox' class context. It accepts optional connection options and returns a Promise that resolves to the sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Define TemplateException in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.3/exceptions/page.mdx

Specifically raised when a sandbox template uses an outdated version of the environment that is incompatible with the current SDK. This helps in managing template versioning issues.

```python
class TemplateException(SandboxException)

```

### Get Sandbox Information using E2B SDK (Python) - Class Method

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Retrieves information about the sandbox, including its ID, template, metadata, and start/end times. This is a class method variant.

```python
from e2b import SandboxInfo, ApiParams

# Assuming 'api' is an instance of the E2B API client
# sandbox_info: SandboxInfo = api.get_info()

@class_method_variant("_cls_get_info")
def get_info(**opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo
```

### Create Sandbox using E2B Python SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the E2B Python SDK. Supports custom templates, timeouts, metadata, environment variables, and network access control. This method is a class method and should be used instead of the constructor.

```python
import os
from typing import Dict, Optional
from unpack import Unpack

# Assuming ApiParams and Self are defined elsewhere in the SDK
class ApiParams:
    pass

class Self:
    pass

class Sandbox:
    @classmethod
    def beta_create(
        cls,
        template: Optional[str] = None,
        timeout: Optional[int] = None,
        auto_pause: bool = False,
        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
        secure: bool = True,
        allow_internet_access: bool = True,
        **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]
    ) -> Self:
        """
        [BETA] Create a new sandbox.

        By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

        Arguments:
            template: Sandbox template name or ID
            timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. The maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
            auto_pause: Automatically pause the sandbox after the timeout expires. Defaults to False.
            metadata: Custom metadata for the sandbox
            envs: Custom environment variables for the sandbox
            secure: Envd is secured with access token and cannot be used without it, defaults to True.
            allow_internet_access: Allow sandbox to access the internet, defaults to True.

        Returns:
            A Sandbox instance for the new sandbox
        """
        # Implementation details would go here
        print("Creating sandbox...")
        return Sandbox() # Placeholder return

# Example usage:
# new_sandbox = Sandbox.beta_create(template="default", timeout=600, allow_internet_access=False)

```

### TypeScript: Define InvalidArgumentError

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the InvalidArgumentError class, thrown when an invalid argument is provided to a function or method. It takes a string message in its constructor.

```typescript
class InvalidArgumentError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    // ... implementation

--------------------------------

### create (default template)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template.

```APIDOC
## create (default template)

### Description
Create a new sandbox from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
- **opts** (object) - Optional - Connection options.
  - **requestTimeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Request timeout in milliseconds.
  - **env** (object) - Optional - Environment variables to set in the sandbox.
  - **cpu** (string) - Optional - CPU type for the sandbox (e.g., 'standard', 'large').
  - **memory** (string) - Optional - Memory size for the sandbox (e.g., 'standard', 'large').
  - **disk** (string) - Optional - Disk size for the sandbox (e.g., 'standard', 'large').

### Request Body
This endpoint does not have a request body.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "opts": {
    "requestTimeoutMs": 30000,
    "env": {
      "MY_VAR": "my_value"
    }
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **instance** (object) - The sandbox instance details.
  - **sandboxId** (string) - The ID of the sandbox.
  - **ipAddress** (string) - The IP address of the sandbox.
  - **status** (string) - The current status of the sandbox (e.g., 'running', 'stopped').

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-12345",
  "instance": {
    "sandboxId": "sandbox-12345",
    "ipAddress": "192.168.1.100",
    "status": "running"
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new sandbox instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.6.0/sandbox/page.mdx

The static `create()` method on the `Sandbox` class provides a way to instantiate new sandbox environments. It can create a sandbox from the default 'desktop' template or a specified template by name or ID, with optional connection `opts`.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')

```

--------------------------------

### Define NotFoundError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.3.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the NotFoundError class, signifying that a requested resource could not be located. It accepts a string message for context.

```typescript
class NotFoundError {
  constructor(message: string): NotFoundError
}
```

--------------------------------

### E2B SDK Error Classes

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.2/errors/page.mdx

Defines custom error classes for various exceptions encountered in the E2B SDK. These errors help in identifying and handling specific failure scenarios like authentication issues, invalid arguments, resource not found, rate limiting, and sandbox-related problems.

```typescript
class AuthenticationError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "AuthenticationError";
  }
}

class InvalidArgumentError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "InvalidArgumentError";
  }
}

class NotEnoughSpaceError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "NotEnoughSpaceError";
  }
}

class NotFoundError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "NotFoundError";
  }
}

class RateLimitError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "RateLimitError";
  }
}

class SandboxError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "SandboxError";
  }
}

class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
  }
}

class TimeoutError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TimeoutError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. This static method requires the 'Sandbox' class context and accepts optional connection options. It returns a Promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string,
   opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize and Use E2B Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.5/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Demonstrates how to instantiate the Sandbox class to create a new isolated cloud environment. The Sandbox class provides access to Linux OS, file management, command execution, and internet access. It requires the 'e2b' library. The default 'base' template is used if none is specified.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox()
```

--------------------------------

### create() - Default Template

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template.

```APIDOC
## create() - Default Template

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template.

### Method
`POST`

### Endpoint
`/sandboxes` (Conceptual)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional. Connection options for the sandbox.

### Request Example
```ts
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create();
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **InstanceType<S>** - A sandbox instance for the newly created sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-789",
  "status": "running"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Generate SDK Reference TOCs and Routes (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/README.md

A Node.js script run as a prebuild step to generate the Table of Contents (TOCs) and routes for the SDK reference documentation. This script ensures the documentation structure is up-to-date before building or running the development server. It can also be executed manually.

```javascript
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const SDK_REF_DIR = path.join(__dirname, '../src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference');
const TOC_FILE = path.join(SDK_REF_DIR, 'toc.json');

function generateToc() {
  const modules = {};
  // Placeholder for logic to scan directories and find modules
  // Example: fs.readdirSync(SDK_REF_DIR).forEach(packageDir => {
  //   // ... recursively scan package versions and modules
  // });

  const tocData = {
    // ... structure for TOC
  };

  fs.writeFileSync(TOC_FILE, JSON.stringify(tocData, null, 2));
  console.log('SDK reference TOC generated successfully.');
}

// This script would typically be run via 'npm run prebuild'
// For manual execution:
if (require.main === module) {
  generateToc();
}

module.exports = generateToc;

```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options include specifying the path, configuration file, team ID, and flags to select templates interactively or skip confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the `create` class method. Supports custom templates, timeouts, environment variables, and API key authentication. Use this instead of the constructor.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(template='my-template', timeout=600, envs={'VAR': 'value'}, api_key='your_api_key')
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js Packages at Runtime (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Installs Node.js packages like 'cowsay' during the sandbox runtime using `sbx.commands.run`. It then shows how to use the installed package within the sandbox.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create()
sbx.commands.run('npm install cowsay') // This will install the cowsay package
sbx.runCode(`
  const cowsay = require('cowsay')
  console.log(cowsay.say({ text: 'Hello, world!' }))
`, { language: 'javascript' })
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox with AsyncSandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Instantiates a new sandbox environment. Supports customization via template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, domain, and debug flags. It is recommended to use this class method over direct instantiation.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(
    template='my-template',
    timeout=600,
    metadata={'user_id': '123'},
    envs={'MY_VAR': 'my_value'},
    api_key='YOUR_API_KEY',
    domain='e2b.dev',
    debug=True,
    request_timeout=10.0
)
```

--------------------------------

### Get Sandbox Metrics using JavaScript SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/metrics/page.mdx

Retrieves CPU, memory, and disk usage metrics for a sandbox using the E2B JavaScript SDK. Metrics are collected every 5 seconds and returned as an array of timestamped objects. Ensure the sandbox has been running for a few seconds to collect initial data.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = await Sandbox.create()
console.log('Sandbox created', sbx.sandboxId)

// Wait for a few seconds to collect some metrics
await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 10_000))

const metrics = await sbx.getMetrics() // $HighlightLine

// You can also get the metrics by sandbox ID:
// const metrics = await Sandbox.getMetrics(sbx.sandboxId)

console.log('Sandbox metrics:', metrics)

// Sandbox metrics:
// [
//   {
//     timestamp: 2025-07-28T08:04:05.000Z,
//     cpuUsedPct: 20.33,
//     cpuCount: 2,
//     memUsed: 32681984,  // in bytes
//     memTotal: 507592704,  // in bytes
//     diskUsed: 1514856448,  // in bytes
//     diskTotal: 2573185024  // in bytes
//   },
//   {
//     timestamp: 2025-07-28T08:04:10.000Z,
//     cpuUsedPct: 0.2,
//     cpuCount: 2,
//     memUsed: 33316864,  // in bytes
//     memTotal: 507592704, // in bytes
//     diskUsed: 1514856448,  // in bytes
//     diskTotal: 2573185024  // in bytes
//   }
// ]
```
